description: **Wondering what’s within Beckman Coulter Diagnostics? Take a closer look.** At first glance, you’ll see that for more than 80 years we’ve been dedicated to advancing and optimising the laboratory to move science and healthcare forward. Join a team where you can be heard, be supported, and always be yourself. We’re building a culture that celebrates backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives of all our associates. Look again and you’ll see we are invested in you, providing the opportunity to build a meaningful career, be creative, and try new things with the support you need to be successful. Beckman Coulter Diagnostics is proud to work alongside a community of six fellow Diagnostics Companies at Danaher. Together, we’re working at the pace of change to improve patient lives with diagnostic tools that address the world’s biggest health challenges. The **Field Applications Specialist (FAS)** at Beckman Coulter Diagnostics is responsible for delivering exceptional customer service by commissioning and supporting our diagnostic instrumentation. This includes leveraging our extensive assay menu, comprehensive training programs, and robust troubleshooting resources to ensure optimal system performance. As part of the European Implementation Team, the FAS will be based in Ireland, supporting local customers and making a direct impact on patient health and overall well\-being. At Beckman Coulter, our vision is to relentlessly re\-imagine healthcare—one diagnosis at a time. You will be a part of the Field Applications Specialist Team and report to the Field Application Manager responsible for providing front line support by resolving customer(s) problems and issues through technical, scientific expertise and troubleshooting. You will help drive revenue growth while ensuring strong customer contract retention rates and warranty conversion. Use critical thinking to resolve application issues, train our customers, verify the operational performance and data quality of systems. If you thrive in a fast\-paced role and want to be part of an extraordinary European post\-sales organisation \- read on. This position will be remote, but candidate will be expected to be on site at Cork and Kerry as a minimum of 4 days per week. **In this role, you will have the opportunity to:** * Develop and build knowledge to understand the industry, the company, the products, tools, and the team. This is supported with a clear Development Pathway program as part our culture to develop and grow our teams. * Be supported by a strong leadership team, involved in keys projects to deliver successful installations for our Customers that meet their requirements and that of our company. * Provide excellent professional support and training to Beckman Coulter’s customers. * Maintain customer support logs and internal records in a timely manner, resolve issues to deliver a high\-quality Customer Experience. * Develop commercial awareness through cross functional support of the business to increase growth opportunities and demonstrate to our Customers our pioneering innovation solutions and new products for the market. **The essential requirements of the job include:** * University Scientific degree \+ proven experience in chemical chemistry and immunoassays laboratory * Ability to interact with customers on a detailed scientific basis to ensure customer needs are fulfilled. * Ability to travel and provide service in assigned geography, as well as periodic travel to other areas and overnight travel. * Ability to work flexible hours as per the business needs. **Travel, Motor Vehicle Record \& Physical/Environment Requirements:** * Hold a current valid driver’s license and good driving record. Associates who drive Beckman Coulter vehicles will follow the Danaher Company Vehicle Policy. FAS operate independently within their geographic territory; however we operate as a team to share standard methodologies, mentor and receive expert technical support. Classified as remote worker, provided with a company car, mobile phone and laptop. Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real\-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life. For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

title: Front of House Receptionist
description: Barden are delighted to be partnering with a leading global technology company to recruit a Workplace Associate, Front of House for their Dublin office. This is an excellent opportunity to join a collaborative workplace team in a people\-facing role where no two days are the same. You’ll play a key part in creating a welcoming, seamless, and high\-quality office experience within a fast\-paced international environment. **Location:** Dublin City Centre **Type:** Full\-time \| Permanent \| On\-site The Opportunity In this role, you will: * Be the first point of contact for employees, visitors, and vendors, delivering a professional front\-of\-house experience * Manage reception operations including visitor sign\-ins, deliveries, access requests, and general office coordination * Support the smooth day\-to\-day running of the workplace, ensuring the office is maintained to a high standard * Assist with internal events, meeting set\-ups, and workplace logistics * Work closely with the Workplace Manager to help create a positive employee experience About You * Experience in front\-of\-house, hospitality, workplace, office support, or customer service * Friendly, organised, and proactive with strong communication skills * Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment * A hands\-on team player who enjoys creating a positive workplace atmosphere This is a fantastic opportunity for someone who enjoys a people\-focused role and wants to join a modern, dynamic, and highly collaborative workplace environment. For more information, please contact **Phonsie Irwin (phonsie.irwin@barden.ie)** or apply via the link below.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer
description: Bentley Systems **Full Stack Software Engineer** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Position Summary** We have a great position for an experienced Full Stack Software Engineer II to join the Integrations \& Interoperability group at Bentley Systems. This group is responsible for advancing integrations and interoperability between various Bentley products. As a Full Stack Software Engineer II, you will work in small, focused, and empowered teams to deliver high\-quality, full\-stack solutions that will be used and loved by our users. You will participate in transforming our industry\-leading products on their journey to leveraging cloud services and solutions. **Responsibilities** * Develop, maintain, and enhance full\-stack interoperability solutions between various products, working on both front\-end and back\-end components. * Take ownership of the functionality you write and the designs you implement across the full technology stack. * Research, evaluate, and explore new technologies as needed to solve complex problems. * Support, enhance, and maintain existing solutions, ensuring their stability and performance. * Develop comprehensive unit, integration, and system tests to ensure code assets are of high quality and satisfy all use cases. * Create and maintain documentation for the solutions you help build. * Contribute as a key member of a small, multi\-disciplinary, self\-organizing agile team, participating in technical designs and decisions. **Qualifications** * 3\-5\+ years of experience designing and implementing high\-quality software. * Solid understanding of programming concepts and paradigms such as OOP and SOLID. * Experience building and maintaining web services and APIs. * Hands\-on experience with TypeScript/React for front\-end development and .NET for back\-end development. * Experience with modern development tools like Microsoft Visual Studio, Git, and Azure Pipelines. * Familiarity with modern test and mocking frameworks. * A strong drive to work in and with a diverse team to deliver advanced software. * BSc in Computer Science or a related field. **What We Offer** * A great Team and culture – please see our colleague video. * An exciting career as an integral part of a world\-leading software company providing solutions for architecture, engineering, and construction \- watch this short documentary about how we got our start. * An attractive salary and benefits package. * A commitment to inclusion, belonging, and colleague well\-being through global initiatives and resource groups. * A company committed to making a real difference by advancing the world’s infrastructure for a better quality of life, where your contributions help build a more sustainable, connected, and resilient world. Discover our latest user success stories for an insight into our global impact.  **About Bentley Systems** Around the world, infrastructure professionals rely on software from Bentley Systems to help them design, build, and operate better and more resilient infrastructure for transportation, water, energy, cities, and more. Founded in 1984 by engineers for engineers, Bentley is the partner of choice for engineering firms and owner\-operators worldwide, with software that spans engineering disciplines, industry sectors, and all phases of the infrastructure lifecycle. Through our digital twin solutions, we help infrastructure professionals unlock the value of their data to transform project delivery and asset performance. www.bentley.com **Equal Opportunity Employer** Bentley is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and considers for employment all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, gender/gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, marital status, religion/belief, national origin, caste, age, or any other characteristic protected by local law or unrelated to job qualifications.

title: Full-Time Sales Assistant with Retail Apprenticeship Opportunity
description: Are you looking for full\-time work while expanding your knowledge and skills in the retail sector? If so, this could be the perfect opportunity for you. O'Brien's Wine has partnered with Retail Skillnet to offer a two\-year Retail Apprenticeship Programme designed for individuals who show the potential to progress into a supervisory or management role within O'Brien's. This programme combines practical, on\-the\-job experience with structured learning, helping you build the skills, attitude, and competencies needed to succeed as a Retail Supervisor or Manager. Throughout the programme, you will: * Work full\-time as a Sales Assistant in our Carrickmines strore * Participate in structured training and development through classroom learning, online modules, and self\-study. * Attend college training for approximately 24 days each year over the two\-year programme. * Gain the knowledge and experience required to achieve a Level 6 qualification in Retail Management. This apprenticeship provides an excellent pathway for individuals who are serious about building a long\-term career in retail management with O'briens while earning and gaining real\-world experience. **Location** Carrickmines **About The Role** At O'Brien's Wine, we're passionate about what we do, and we're looking for a dynamic and enthusiastic Full\-Time Sales Assistant to join our team. If you love great wine, enjoy working with people, and are eager to learn, this role offers the opportunity to develop your retail career while completing a recognised apprenticeship qualification. As a Sales Assistant, you will play a key role in supporting the store's daily operations while delivering exceptional service to our customers. You'll be the face of O'Brien's, helping customers find the perfect wine and ensuring that our store runs smoothly. **Your Responsibilities** * Complete tasks assigned by management to the highest standards while following company policies. * Provide outstanding customer service by greeting and assisting customers with a positive and helpful attitude. * Accurately operate the till system and handle transactions. * Keep stock levels organised and up to date. * Maintain high merchandising standards to ensure the store is always presentable. * Continuously develop your product knowledge, particularly wine, to better assist customers. * Support deliveries, displays, and the overall appearance of the store. * Actively participate in the Retail Apprenticeship Programme, including attending training and completing required coursework. **Who We're Looking For** * Someone who is serious about building a career in retail management. * Eager and willing to learn and develop new skills. * Friendly, enthusiastic, and approachable personality. * A genuine interest in wine and willingness to learn more. * Strong customer service and sales focus. * Hardworking, with a positive attitude and team\-oriented mindset. * Willing to support store operations including deliveries and merchandising. * Must be able to work weekends. * Interested in working while achieving a Level 6 qualification through the apprenticeship programme. **Benefits** **What We Offer You** * Employee discounts on wine and fizz for all colleagues. * Bike to Work scheme. * Access to HSF Health Plans covering dental, optical, and healthcare expenses. * Company Pension Scheme. * Refer a Friend programme. * Development opportunities through online and classroom\-based learning. **Training \& Growth** * Opportunity to participate in WSET Wine \& Spirit courses to expand your wine knowledge and gain industry\-recognised qualifications. * Structured development through the Retail Apprenticeship Programme. * Excellent career progression opportunities, with many of our promotions filled internally. **Our Commitment to Inclusion** At O'Brien's Wine we believe every colleague should feel empowered to be their authentic self at work\-respected, valued, and included, no matter who they are, where they're based, or what role they play. Inclusion is at the heart of how we operate. When we embrace individuality and diverse perspectives, we foster a sense of belonging\-and diverse teams make better decisions and drive greater impact. As a company, we are continuously expanding, but it's the dedication and talent of our people that drives our success. We believe in fostering talent and providing ongoing learning opportunities to help you advance in your career. If you're motivated, ready to learn, and excited about working in a vibrant and busy environment, there's plenty of room to grow with us.

title: GDPR & FOI Officer - Financial Services
description: A candidate with 1\-2 years Data Protection (GDPR and FOI) experience is being sought by a well\-known financial services firm. This is a newly created role which will offer a lot of autonomy and requires a candidate with a strong ability to manage their own workload and take initiative. ***You will need to be strong on the IT side of data protection and be able to demonstrate a strong understanding of data protection frameworks.*** Suitable candidates for this role will have: * 1\+ years’ experience in Data Governance, Data Control or Data Protection. * Experience handling FOI and Data Subject Access requests. * Strong understanding of Data Protection frameworks and legislation particularly as it stands within the Financial Service sector. * Excellent analytical skills including the ability to interpret large data sets, conduct gap analyses, identify relevant issues and to suggest risk\-based solutions. This is a permanent role with a hybrid office arrangement. The offices are in Dublin 2, only minutes from the Green Line Luas and several bus routes. The duties in this role will vary according to business need, projects and ongoing policy improvements but you will be responsible for: * Upkeep and promotion of Data Protection standards across the business in Ireland, including the production of internal communications and guidance on best practice. * Identify areas for improvement in data across the business and development of strategies. * Coordination of Data Subject Access Requests, GDPR queries, and Records of Processing Activities. * Preparation of monthly reporting for management, development of monitoring controls and conducting of audits to ensure continued best practice. If you feel ready for a role with lots of responsibility and the chance to work with a global firm please submit your CV today.

title: Graduate Architect
description: **Graduate Architect – 1–3 Years’ Irish Experience Required** An established Irish architectural practice is seeking a Graduate Architect to join its growing studio. The team consists of approximately 26 architects and design professionals with over two decades of experience delivering projects across residential, masterplanning, public, and commercial sectors. **The Role** You will work alongside experienced architects on a variety of housing\-led projects across Ireland, contributing through all RIBA work stages. The position offers strong exposure to large\-scale residential developments and complex planning frameworks. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the preparation of planning drawings, design packages, and presentation material * Support project delivery across residential and mixed\-use schemes * Collaborate with senior architects, consultants, and multidisciplinary teams * Participate in design development, technical detailing, and project coordination * Contribute to planning submissions and compliance documentation **Requirements** * Degree in Architecture (Part I or Part II) * 1–3 years’ professional experience in Ireland is essential * Strong understanding of Irish planning and building regulations * Proficiency in Revit and Adobe Suite preferred * Excellent communication and organisational skills * Ability to work within a collaborative studio environment **What’s on Offer** * Opportunity to work on high\-profile residential and masterplanning projects * Supportive studio with experienced design professionals * Exposure to projects across public and private sectors * Career development within a growing architectural practice To apply or learn more, contact directly via LinkedIn with your CV and portfolio.

title: Graduate Business Development Executive
description: **Overview** We are seeking a motivated and ambitious Business Development Executive to join our clients growing team. based in Dundalk, this role offers hands\-on experience across multiple business functions and is an excellent opportunity for a student or recent graduate looking to gain real\-world experience in a fast\-paced environment. **Key Duties \& Responsibilities** * Identify key customers and build strong relationships to generate new sales opportunities and support year\-on\-year sales growth. * Develop and implement marketing plans to reach target audiences through social media, conferences, exhibitions, and other channels. * Engage proactively with customers to generate leads, track projects, promote new business lines, and drive sales performance. * Champion the needs of new customers and ensure a positive customer experience. * Manage the new customer sales pipeline and support incremental sales targets. * Prepare regular reports on sales and business development activities, including progress against key performance indicators (KPIs). * Gather and provide competitor intelligence to support product costing, market development, and product positioning strategies. * Provide feedback on issues that may impact customer relationships and business performance. * Collaborate with internal teams to support the development of innovative products and service offerings. * Work closely with Marketing and Product Management teams to develop campaigns for new technologies and solutions. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Currently studying or recently graduated in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field. * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to multitask and meet deadlines. * Analytical mindset with an interest in data and reporting; experience with Google Analytics or similar tools is beneficial. * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * Experience with CRM or CMS platforms is an advantage. ***For a confidential conversation about the role please apply for the role or contact Owen Clancy at oclancy@sterlingengineeringeu.com By applying to this job post you agree that Sterling Engineering may process your personal data for recruitment purpose***

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer – Power and Renewables
description: Graduate Electrical Engineer \- Power \& Renewables **About Kirby Group Engineering** Kirby Group Engineering is a leading international engineering services contractor, delivering Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation solutions across Ireland, the UK and Europe. We work with major clients in fast‑growing sectors including power transmission, renewables, data centres and life sciences, and are known for our strong safety culture, technical excellence and focus on developing people. **Power \& Renewables at Kirby** Kirby Is a Market Leader In Power And Renewables, Delivering HV And MV Design And Build Projects Up To 400kV, Including AIS And GIS Substations, Grid Connections And Renewable Infrastructure. Our Teams Work On * HV/MV substations and grid connections * HVDC interconnector projects * Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) * Electrical and civil Balance of Plant works * We have connected over 2GW of renewable energy to the grid in Ireland and the UK and deliver projects for organisations such as ESB Networks, EirGrid, TSOs, wind developers and major industrial clients. **The Graduate Programme** The Kirby Electrical Engineering Graduate Programme is a 24‑month programme designed to kick‑start your career in Power \& Renewables. **You Will** * * Be supported by a dedicated mentor * Work on live HV, MV and renewables projects * Complete structured rotations across Engineering, EHS, Quality, Planning and Commercial teams * Receive high‑quality technical, personal and commercial training through the Kirby Academy * On successful completion, graduates progress to Electrical Project Engineer. **The Role** As a Graduate Electrical Engineer in Power \& Renewables, you will support the delivery of electrical works on major infrastructure projects. You’ll gain hands‑on site experience while developing your engineering, safety and commercial awareness. **What You’ll Be Involved In** * Supporting electrical installation works on HV/MV and renewables projects * Reviewing drawings, specifications and technical submissions * Assisting with site inspections, snagging and coordination * Supporting procurement, material tracking and supplier engagement * Participating in testing, commissioning and energisation activities * Maintaining quality, safety and progress documentation * Working closely with project teams, subcontractors and clients **Essential** **What We’re Looking For** * Level 8 Degree in Electrical Engineering or related discipline * Good understanding of electrical engineering fundamentals * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings *Desirable* * Internship or placement experience * Interest in power systems, renewables, substations or energy infrastructure * Familiarity with AutoCAD **About You** * Motivated, curious and keen to learn * Strong problem‑solving and communication skills * Enjoy working as part of a team * Organised, professional and safety‑focused

title: Graduate Electrical Services Engineers 2026
description: **Graduate Electrical Services Engineer 2026** **Position** : Electrical Services Graduate Engineers \- 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Malmo, Groningen \& Charleroi **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** * Review and understand project documents (specifications, drawings etc). * Supporting with design reviews and analysing supplier information for alignment with project documents. * Develop and manage technical submittals, RFIs, procurement registers, trackers and associated administrative duties. * Working with the BIM team to capture all electrical aspects of the project requirements. * Coordination with other trades and disciplines. * Supporting various project meetings as appropriate. * Supporting weekly project reporting. * Supporting QA/QC on site including system walkdowns and project handover protocols. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to data centre, contracting and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. They will also be encouraged and supported to work towards achieving Chartership with Engineers Ireland throughout their career with Jones Engineering. **Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Degree Qualification in an Electrical Engineering discipline. * Valid Safepass / Manual handling certificates required for successful applicants. * Trade background would be advantageous but not necessary. * Experience/knowledge of data centres or construction sector would be advantageous. * Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. * Excellent organisational and planning skills. * Proficiency in MS Office systems and other engineering software. * Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development. **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies for this role.

title: Growth Account Executive (French Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team is the engine that drives new customer acquisition and engagement for Revolut Business across the globe. Each area of the department works like special forces: from prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments with a solution\-oriented approach and use their know\-how to grow our customer base at lightning speed. We’re looking for a Growth Account Executive who’ll leverage their industry knowledge and product expertise to match customers with the best solutions and drive adoption of new features. If you’re a motivated individual who enjoys a challenge and acts with integrity at all times, we’d love to hear from you. **What You'll Be Doing** * Conducting analytical assessment of assigned accounts to uncover opportunities and deepen customer relationships via a solution\-based sales approach * Building rapport with customers by completing discovery check\-ins and follow\-ups * Understanding where each Revolut Business feature fits into the competitive landscape to effectively communicate our value proposition * Meeting and exceeding sales targets in line with the team’s KPIs **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and French * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone and email * 2\+ years of sales/commercial experience, ideally in a B2B environment * The ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment * A proven track record in telesales or account management, demonstrating success in achieving sales targets * Excellent project management and problem\-solving skills * An analytical mindset to spot opportunities that others would miss * Adeptness at building and maintaining client relationships * A deep understanding of Revolut Business products to become the go\-to resource for your team * Advanced experience in pricing strategies and negotiation, beyond basic sales techniques, with the ability to influence stakeholders Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: IT Service Desk Engineer
description: #### **About the Job:** LaunchDarkly is seeking an IT Service Desk Engineer to deliver a world\-class internal technology experience for employees across the company. You'll solve a broad range of technical issues across devices, identity and access, collaboration tools, and business applications, while serving as a trusted partner who helps teams stay productive and secure. Beyond day\-to\-day support, you'll play a key role in improving how IT operates by identifying trends, strengthening documentation, and helping build scalable support processes that elevate the employee experience. #### **Responsibilities:** * Provide day\-to\-day service desk support for employees in region and across the broader organization, including troubleshooting hardware, operating systems, collaboration platforms, SaaS applications, access issues, and common workplace technology problems. * Own support tickets from intake through resolution, including triage, root\-cause investigation, communication of status, documentation of actions taken, and timely handoff or escalation when needed. * Diagnose issues of diverse scope by evaluating identifiable factors, gathering relevant information, and selecting practical solutions based on precedent, judgment, and user impact. * Support identity and access management processes, including provisioning, deprovisioning, access changes, and troubleshooting authentication or permissions issues in accordance with company policies and security requirements. * Configure, deploy, maintain, and troubleshoot laptops, peripherals, meeting room technology, mobile device workflows, and standard employee productivity tools. * Create, maintain, and improve internal documentation, knowledge base articles, troubleshooting guides, and repeatable operating procedures so employees and teammates can resolve common issues more efficiently. * Identify recurring incidents or service gaps, recommend improvements, and help implement changes that reduce friction, improve reliability, and strengthen the employee experience. * Partner with Security, People Operations, Finance, Workplace, and other internal teams to coordinate onboarding, offboarding, access requests, device logistics, and issue resolution. * Contribute to operational readiness for office or remote support needs in region, including inventory accuracy, asset lifecycle support, and local coordination for employee technology needs. * Advise employees on effective use of workplace technology in a clear, respectful, and accessible way, adapting communication style to differing audiences as expected of a P3 professional. #### **Qualifications:** * Demonstrated ability to independently resolve a wide range of IT support issues across laptops, operating systems, endpoint configuration, collaboration tools, SaaS applications, and account access workflows in a business environment. * Demonstrated ability to manage a queue of support requests, prioritize effectively, document work clearly, and follow issues through to resolution while maintaining a high standard of customer service. * Working knowledge of identity and access management concepts such as account provisioning, group\-based access, SSO, MFA, and permissions troubleshooting. * Experience supporting macOS and/or Windows environments, common productivity and collaboration platforms, and standard endpoint support processes. * Ability to investigate technical issues by gathering facts, identifying patterns, evaluating options, and choosing an appropriate path forward when information is incomplete. * Ability to communicate technical concepts, status updates, and recommendations clearly to employees and stakeholders with different levels of technical familiarity. * Ability to build effective working relationships with internal partners and maintain a calm, respectful, and solutions\-oriented approach during support interactions. * Experience creating or improving support documentation, knowledge articles, or standard operating procedures. * Ability to handle sensitive systems and employee information with sound judgment and appropriate confidentiality. * 5\+ years of related experience with a Bachelor's degree, or equivalent work experience. **About You:** * You are customer\-focused and approach employee support with empathy, clarity, and follow\-through. * You communicate in a direct and respectful way, especially when helping someone who may be frustrated or unfamiliar with the technology involved. * You are organized and reliable in managing multiple requests, documenting your work, and meeting agreed timelines. * You look for patterns in recurring issues and take action to improve tools, documentation, or workflows rather than solving the same problem repeatedly. * You work well independently on day\-to\-day responsibilities and collaborate effectively when an issue requires coordination across teams. * You are adaptable and practical, using sound judgment to navigate varied support situations and changing business needs. #### **About LaunchDarkly:** Modern software delivery was supposed to be the foundation for a thriving digital business but reality has proven otherwise. Slow, inefficient development cycles, costly outages, and fragmented customer experiences are preventing developers from building their best software. The LaunchDarkly platform helps developers innovate on new features faster while protecting them with a safety valve to instantly rewind when things go wrong. Developers can target product experiences to any customer segment and maximize the business impact of every feature. And by gradually rolling out new application components, they escape nightmare "big\-bang" technology migrations. The LaunchDarkly platform was built to guide engineers to the next frontier of DevOps by: * Improving the velocity and stability of software releases, without the fear of end customer outages * Delivering targeted experiences by easily personalizing features to customer cohorts * Maximizing the business impact of every feature through the ability to experiment and optimize * Coordinating the release and optimization of software to provide consistent experiences across mobile platforms and device types * Improving the effectiveness and productivity of engineering teams, by providing insights into engineering cadence and stability At LaunchDarkly, we believe in the power of teams. We're building a team that is humble, open, collaborative, respectful and kind. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. LD invites any applicant to review our written Affirmative Action Plan. To do so, contact People Ops at hr@launchdarkly.com. **Do you need a disability accommodation?** Fill out this accommodations request form and someone from our People Operations team will contact you for assistance. **Your safety matters to us.** To protect yourself from potential scams, LaunchDarkly recruiters will only contact you from @launchdarkly.com email addresses or via LinkedIn from "Verified Recruiter" accounts. Be cautious of emails from other domains. Legitimate LaunchDarkly recruiters will never ask for money, fees, or banking information before making a job offer. LaunchDarkly will never make a job offer without conducting a formal interview process. Our interview process does not involve asking detailed questions by email. If you are ever unsure about a communication that you receive, don't click any links—visit Careers \| LaunchDarkly directly for confirmed job openings and links to apply. Please notify us of any fraudulent representation by sending an email to careers@launchdarkly.com.

title: Indirect Tax Consultant

This is a dynamic role which will involve the successful candidate working in our tax service line supporting the Indirect Tax management team to deliver Indirect Tax compliance and consulting a variety of significant privately\-owned Irish companies. * Working closing with Partners and senior management in the delivery of high\-end Indirect Tax advisory and consulting services. * Support in the delivery of Indirect Tax function improvement and optimisation through the application of technology solutions. * Liaising with clients in a timely efficient manner, advising, researching, and resolving all relevant Indirect Tax issues and Indirect Tax technical matters on advisory assignments. * Full commercial management for a portfolio of client work. * Working closely with other RSM Business units within the firm to identify Indirect Tax opportunities to provide to existing clients. * Corresponding with Revenue and other third parties on behalf of clients. * Coaching, mentoring, and reviewing workload of associate level trainees including presenting in\-house Indirect Tax training courses. * Contribution to the continued development and growth of the practice. **About The Ideal Candidate** The ideal candidate will have the following skills and experience: * At least 3 years plus experience in Irish Indirect Tax compliance and advisory assignments. * Experience in VAT on property transaction, RCT, due diligence projects and cross\-border transactions desirable but not essential. * Experience working in a Big4/Top10 professional services firm desirable but not essential. * Strong academic record with a degree in accounting, economics, finance, law, or relevant degree. * Part or fully qualified AITI/CTA or equivalent qualification. * Expert at directing and out clients. * Relationship management \- ability to read situations and modify behaviour to build quality, diverse relationships. * Demonstrate an ability to contribute to new ideas and propose innovative solutions to problems. * Excellent communication skills both written and verbal as well as strong attention to detail skills. * Enthusiastic team player and manager with a passion to coach and encourage others to achieve their potential. * Ambitious – demonstrate strong commitment to personal learning and development. ***\#RSM is an equal opportunity employer***

Our organization has grown significantly over the past decade and we are searching for motivated candidates with finance backgrounds to join our team as Investment Counsellors to help us better the financial industry in Ireland as we continue our expansion in Europe. **The Opportunity:** The Investment Counsellor (IC) role is focused on managing relationships with high\-net\-worth clients in Ireland. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any legally protected characteristic.* **Ready To Apply?** Don't leave yourself wondering 'what if'. title: Junior Business Analyst
description: **Job Title:** Business Analyst / Application Support (SmartSimple) **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid) **Contract Type:** Day Rate Contract (1\-year Contract) **Strong Daily Rate** **Start Date:** ASAP (Two Interview Stage Process) **Overview:** PFH Technology is partnering directly with a Dublin based Client who seeks a **Business Analyst / Application Support** with experience in grant management platforms and application support environments. The role involves supporting and configuring business applications, assisting with data migration activities, producing functional documentation, and delivering reporting insights through Power BI. **Mandatory Qualifications:** * Degree level qualification in **Information Technology, Computer Science, Project Management, Finance, or related discipline** **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide efficient **L1/L2 application support** and helpdesk management. * Configure and support **grant management workflows and schemes** within the platform. * Produce and maintain **functional and technical documentation** . * Support **data migration activities** , including validation and testing. * Create and maintain **test scripts and validation reports** . * Support development and maintenance of **Power BI dashboards and reporting outputs** . * Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and clarify business requirements. * Ensure timely resolution of issues and continuous system improvement. * Support system enhancements, user adoption, and operational processes. * Maintain accurate records, reporting, and support documentation. **Must\-Have Skills \& Experience:** * Strong experience with **SmartSimple** or similar **grant management platforms** . * Proven experience in **L1/L2 application support environments** . * Experience as an **IT Business Analyst** within public sector or regulated environments. * Hands\-on experience supporting **data migration projects** . * Knowledge of **Power BI dashboards and reporting development** . * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent **stakeholder engagement and communication skills** . * Experience with **testing, validation, and documentation processes** . * Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities. * Understanding of business processes and system configuration. Please note no visa sponsorship for this role PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway.

title: Junior Consultant - Training Provided

Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €45k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

With nonstop training, you’ll keep developing your skills and growing your career. * **Get Certified:** Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant and build credibility that opens doors. **What You Will Need** * At least 2 year of experience in a sales\-focused role (e.g., Sales Rep, Sales Associate, Account Executive, Business Development, Inside sales, Tele sales representative, Sales Manager, Customer Success Manager, etc.) * An ambitious, growth\-driven mindset * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent is a plus but not required. **Total Compensation (Average Performer)** * Year 1: **€45,000\+** * Year 2: **€75,000\+** * Base salary: DOE * Commission: Uncapped, unlimited earning potential * Top performers earn significantly above these figures Ready for a new, exciting opportunity? Contact Maria de Gouveia at maria.degouveia@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+353 1 572 1202 to learn more about this thrilling opportunity. Take your career to new heights!

title: Junior Cybersecurity Consultant
description: **Job Title:** Junior Cybersecurity Consultant **Location:** Dublin 3 **Job Type:** Permanent **Work Type:** Hybrid **Role Type:** Cybersecurity **Overview of Role** Ergo are seeking a Junior Cybersecurity Consultant to join our market\-leading Cybersecurity Team in a hybrid role based at our Eastpoint offices in Dublin 3\. The successful candidate will collaborate with established team members to assess IT Systems, Infrastructure, Policies, and Cybersecurity measures for a broad range of customers, determining their risk posture and advising on appropriate mitigation measures and Cybersecurity road\-maps. This is an excellent opportunity to gain experience from some of the best in the business while developing your own consultancy skills. **Role \& Responsibilities:** * Assist in Cybersecurity assessments designed to evaluate an organisation's security posture using established frameworks such as CIS, NIST, and ISO 27001\. * Support Penetration testing activities, including steering the activity, conducting walkthroughs of results, and providing remediation paths to customers. * Inform the business on emerging threats and advise on mitigation strategies. * Support compliance activity including ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials, Secure Score and MCSB, advise on monitoring and provide opportunities for continual improvement initiatives. * Build risk universes that account for inherent and residual risk profiles, considering control measures as well as the risks themselves. * Advise on Security Training and Awareness programs. * Ensure legislative conformity with respect to GDPR, Cloud Act, NIS2, DORA, AI Act, and other relevant regulations. * Maintain technical documentation and how\-to guides. * Work closely with SOC Engineers to support SOC/SIEM service delivery. * Advise on appropriate security tooling solutions. * Flexibility to travel to project sites, industry events, and trade shows as required. **Skills, Qualifications \& Experience** * Minimum 2\+ years in a similar Cybersecurity role. * Proven experience in conducting compliance assessments and collaborating on governance initiatives. * Comprehensive and up\-to\-date understanding of frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST, CIS and associated regulations/directives such as NIS2, DORA, AI Act \& GDPR. * Exceptional analytical and critical thinking skills, enabling thorough evaluation of complex data and development of effective policies. * Excellent communication skills, with the ability to convey technical information clearly to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Strong leadership and collaboration capabilities and the ability to manage relationships effectively. **Experience:** * Experience using a ticketing or ITSM system (for example ServiceNow or similar). * Experience supporting Penetration testing activities and providing remediation guidance. * Experience working with compliance frameworks and security governance initiatives. **Education \& Certifications:** * Degree\-level qualification in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, Information Security, or an equivalent IT\-related discipline. * Relevant industry certifications considered advantageous. **Visa:** * Irish/EU Citizen or Stamp 4 Visa holder. **About Us** As Ireland's largest IT Solutions Provider with a growing global footprint, Ergo offers deep expertise across managed IT \& hardware services, cloud, applications \& software services, IT resourcing, and modern work solutions. Our recent technology awards reflect our commitment to delivering industry\-leading IT solutions, highlighting our team's dedication and expertise.

title: Junior Data Engineer, Economics
description: **About Chainlink** Chainlink is the industry\-standard oracle platform bringing the capital markets onchain and powering the majority of decentralized finance (DeFi). The Chainlink stack provides the essential data, interoperability, compliance, and privacy standards needed to power advanced blockchain use cases for institutional tokenized assets, lending, payments, stablecoins, and more. Since inventing decentralized oracle networks, Chainlink has enabled tens of trillions in transaction value and now secures the vast majority of DeFi. Many of the world’s largest financial services institutions have also adopted Chainlink’s standards and infrastructure, including Swift, Euroclear, Mastercard, Fidelity International, UBS, S\&P Dow Jones Indices, FTSE Russell, WisdomTree, ANZ, and top protocols such as Aave, Lido, GMX and many others. Chainlink leverages a novel fee model where offchain and onchain revenue from enterprise adoption is converted to LINK tokens and stored in a strategic Chainlink Reserve. Learn more at chain.link. Chainlink Labs is pioneering the world’s first fully sustainable and decentralized oracle network, and the Economics team is searching for a Junior Data Engineer to play a critical role in building it. This role will sit at the intersection of blockchain technology, financial innovation, and economic systems, and shape the future of decentralized economics. As a Junior Data Engineer on the Economics team, you will combine data science, economics, and blockchain expertise to shape incentives, optimize network design, and influence the decision\-making of our Economics and Executive Leadership teams. You will design, analyze, and operationalize economic models for production applications. You will collaborate across departments to bring innovative solutions from concept to production, ensuring technical and economic soundness and throughout. This is a high\-impact role, offering visibility and influence as you contribute to the development of the Chainlink network. If you’re a meticulous, creative, and motivated individual ready to solve complex economic problems through data\-driven decisions in the rapidly evolving world of Web3, this is the role for you. **Your Impact** * You will serve two primary functions: (1\) designing, building, and maintaining scalable data pipelines and infrastructure to help the Economics and Executive Leadership teams make informed, data\-driven decisions, and (2\) building and maintaining production economics applications. * You will work on a wide array of blockchain economics projects, analyses, and systems, including: + Designing novel mechanisms and incentive schemes + Building data pipelines, models, and simulations to evaluate investment opportunities + Designing and parameterizing economics systems + Optimizing parameters for production applications + Contributing to the design of Economics programs and applications * You will optimize network incentives and systems by engaging directly with the Economics, Product, Engineering, Research, and Data Science teams to understand network dynamics at the deepest level. * You will meticulously operationalize processes, and coordinate cross\-functionally to implement them. * This is both a highly technical and highly social role. You will have high visibility and a large impact on how Chainlink builds the world’s first fully sustainable decentralized oracle network. **Requirements** * A deep understanding of financial and crypto markets and incentives * Strong foundation in Computer Science principles, including data structures, algorithms, and complexity analysis * Experience with data infrastructure design and implementation in cloud platforms (e.g., GCP, AWS, Azure) * Experience with production\-grade software engineering concepts (e.g. git, testing, containerization, CI/CD, infrastructure as code) * Strong SQL and Python skills are mandatory * Substantial data analysis and/or data science experience * Meticulous attention to detail **Preferred Qualifications** * Strong organizational and coordination skills necessary to productionize and operationalize processes and applications * Strong Computer Science or related Engineering, Economics and/or Finance background through Masters\-level academics and/or multiple years of industry experience * A proven interest in DeFi and in remaining current with new products and trends * Experience with LLM\-based development tools, onchain data, and orchestration tools such as Airflow and DBT * Experience in data modeling, data warehousing concepts * Published research with an economics or crypto focus (e.g., papers on MEV or PBS, staking and slashing dynamics, node compensation, tokenomics, etc.) **Competencies** * You must be meticulous, creative, and hard\-working. * You should be a curious, collaborative self\-starter. * You’re ideally growth oriented, self\-aware, and willing to be wrong. *All roles with Chainlink Labs are global and remote\-based. Unless otherwise stated, we ask that you try to overlap some working hours with Eastern Standard Time (EST).* *We carefully review all applications and aim to provide a response to every candidate within two weeks after the job posting closes. **The closing date is listed on the job advert, so we encourage you to take the time to thoughtfully prepare your application.** We want to fully consider your experience and skills, and you will hear from us regarding the status of your application shortly after the closing date.* **Commitment to Equal Opportunity** Chainlink Labs is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment in compliance with applicable laws, regulations, or ordinances. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need when applying for a role or in our recruitment process, please contact us via this form. **Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants** Information collected and processed as part of your Chainlink Labs Careers profile, and any job applications you choose to submit, is subject to our Recruiting Privacy Policy. By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required.

title: Junior Electrical Estimator

Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Responsibilities Of The Role** * As part of an established team of Mechanical \& Electrical professionals the successful candidate will be involved in the production of competitive tenders/detailed estimates assisting the existing estimators and beginning with the process of getting quantities from plans and models under supervision. * Compiling specialist sub\-contract quotes. * Ensuring sub\-contractor quotes comply with specification. * Completing take\-offs from tender drawings and models. * Filing and storing of all information in current folder system. * Training in the mechanical role to become proficient in M\&E. **Your Qualifications And Key Criteria** * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. * Highly numerate. * Computer literacy in relevant estimating packages. * Willingness to learn. * Available to attend head office full time for the first three months at least. Hybrid working will be acceptable after this initial period. **Desirable Skills** * A previous trades or engineering background. * Microsoft Excel. ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: Junior Merchandiser
description: ### **About The Role** We are currently recruiting for a Junior Merchandiser to be based from our offices in Clonee. The role offers flexibility to work on a hybrid basis. Our mission across Supply Chain and Merchandising is to identify and support opportunities to optimise store availability and stock levels to fully maximise Poundland \& Dealz sales potential whilst controlling the costs of the business. This is delivered through focusing on simplifying how we work as a team and across the wider business by optimising our systems and driving consistent and collaborative ways of working across our teams and business partners. The Junior Merchandiser will be responsible for supporting the coordination of outbound General Merchandise replenishment and inbound planning to maximise store availability and sales optimisation across Poundland \& Dealz stores. As Junior Merchandiser, you will: * Support Core and Promotional Outbound Replenishment alongside the Branch Merchandising Team * Ensure Core GM Availability targets are met in line with KPI * Manage Seasonal Outbound Replenishment * Manage stock allocation across multiple warehouses in UK and ROI to support efficient distribution to stores * Commercial and Merchandising Collaborative Planning ensuring contingency plans are agreed in advance to optimize store availability and minimize gaps during sell through periods * Identify and resolve stock\-related issues, ensuring minimal disruption to supply * Collaborate closely with the Merchandising team to align on inbound stock deliveries and priorities * Ensure Launch availability for Seasonal Events across distribution centres, avoiding overstocking and stock shortages * Monitor inbound inventory across distribution centres and regions, assessing availability by location and country, agitate and interrogate any gaps in availability or Range Plans * Manage the flow of stock into ROI, ensuring efficient allocation aligned with store demand * Execute inter\-depot transfers where required, optimising stock positioning rather than relying solely on direct UK\-to\-store distribution * Evaluate and select the most effective distribution routes, balancing DC picks and transfers to maximise efficiency We are looking for someone who will: * Be a technical specialist with operational knowledge of Supply chain (desired) * Have and understanding of how Supply Chain systems work (desired) * Have experience of working with Supply Chain replenishment systems (desired) * Have experience working in a fast\-paced supply chain, retail, or logistics environment. * Have great demonstrable collaborative working ethic. * Have ability to solve problems and make decisions, as well as to think * strategically and laterally * Be able to communicate and influence effectively at all business levels. * Have a key eye for detail and ability to analyse data. * Have a relevant qualification in business, supply chain, logistics and business change (desired) We offer rewards that go beyond the payslip, including: * 10% employee discount card with increased discount events over the year. * Celebrate your birthday, you’ll get an extra day off for your birthday. * 30 days holiday a year inc banks, rising by one day a year up to 33 days. * Annual bonus scheme, with the opportunity to earn up to 10% of your base salary. * We put wellbeing first through our partnership with Retail Trust which gives you and your family access to a fantastic range of resources. * My Staff Shop, giving you an amazing number of discounts and shopping vouchers. * For those unexpected moments, our partnership with stream enables you to access up to 50% of your monthly earned wages early, along with savings and financial advice. * Training and development opportunities, we want you to grow your career with us, be that through one of the qualifications we offer or internal programmes, this role can be just the first step. * Plus, life assurance, loyalty service awards and NAERSA pension. **Bring your energy, teamwork, and get stuck in approach — and we’ll help you build your career with us!** We’re committed and proud to our inclusive culture, and that starts with the recruitment and selection process. If you require a reasonable adjustment during the recruitment process, please do share this with us on your application form and we’ll make every effort to meet your needs.
